Off Calendar Jan-14-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Mary Buddle as wrongful death heir and as successor-in-interest to Ellory Buddle, deceased, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT BORGWARNER MORSE TEC INC. AS SUCCESSOR-BY-MERGER TO BORG-WARNER CORPORATION'S MOTION TO BIFURCATE TRIAL ON THE ISSUE OF MEDICAL CAUSATION

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant BorgWarner Morse Tec LLC's motion to bifurcate trial on the issue of medical causation is denied without prejudice to being raised before the trial judge. As to plaintiffs' argument that the motion is untimely, the Court initially agreed with plaintiffs' argument that the motion is untimely, but after briefing on this motion was complete, on January 12, 2016, this Court approved the parties' stipulation and order to continue the trial date and to reopen limited expert discovery. As the trial date is now April 18, 2016, the motion is timely pursuant to C.C.P. 598. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Kimberly R. Hendershott of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12552 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

Notice Of Motion To Reconsider Order Deeming Admissions Admitted [Ccp 1008]; Or In The Alternative, To Allow For The Withdrawal Or Amendment Of Admissions [Ccp 2033.300]
Off Calendar Oct-13-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Autumn Mesa of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Mary Buddle as wrongful death heir and as successor in interest to Ellory Buddle, deceased, et al.
Stephanie A. Huang of DEHAY ELLISTON LLP (510-285-0750), appearing for defendant / opposing party Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO RECONSIDER ORDER DEEMING ADMISSIONS ADMITTED PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 1008(A); OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE TO ALLOW FOR THE WITHDRAWAL OR AMENDMENT OF ADMISSONS [CCP2033.300]

Prior to the ruling the Court took notice of defendant's objection to Brayton Purcell's representation of Tamara Ylasyuk. Hearing held. The Court made the following ruling: Plaintiffs' motion to reconsider order deeming admissions admitted pursuant to C.C.P. 1008(a) is granted. Plaintiffs demonstrated new or different facts to warrant reconsideration. Upon reconsideration, the Court vacates its September 2, 2015 order granting Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion to deem admitted the requests for admission it propounded upon plaintiff Tamara Vlasyuk. The Court now denies Kaiser Gypsum's motion upon finding that before the September 2, 2015 hearing, Tamara served a proposed response to the requests for admission that was in substantial compliance with C.C.P. section 2033.220. (C.C.P. 2033.280(c).) Kaiser Gypsum's request for sanctions is denied. Discovery is reopened for the limited purpose of the deposition of plaintiff Tamara Vlasyuk, which deposition must conclude no later than December 4, 2015. Plaintiff waives all objections and must respond to RFA #13, 14, and 15. This limited reopening of discovery closes December 7, 2015. All other discovery remains closed. Counsel for plaintiffs to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Sandy Lee of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 971 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Sep-10-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kevin Barger of DEHAY ELLISTON LLP (510-285-0750), appearing for defendant / moving party Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.
Geoff T. Sloniker of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Mary Buddle as wrongful death heir and as successor-in-interest to Ellory Buddle, deceased, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication as to the claims of plaintiffs Mary Buddle and Cheryl Ahrens is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent Ellory Buddle was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Kim Richardson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #5915 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Deeming Admitted And Establishing The Truth Of All Matters Specified In Requests For Admissions; Sanctions
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kevin T. Barger of Dehay Elliston (510.285.0747), appearing for defendant / moving party Kaiser Gypsum.
Kimberly Chu of Brayton Purcell (415.898.1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Tamara Vlasyuk.

RULING:
DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR AN ORDER DEEMING ADMITTED AND ESTABLISHING THE TRUTH OF ALL MATTERS SPECIFIED IN REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS.

Matter called. Discussions/argument held with regard to the electronic filing of the Request for Admissions, Ms. Chu's appearance before the Court today, and Brayton Purcell's withdrawal as counsel of record as to Plaintiff Tamara Vlasyuk. Further argument held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion to deem admitted and establish the truth of all matters specified in its requests for admissions to plaintiff Tamara Vlasyuk is granted. Ms. Vlasyuk did not serve a response to the requests for admission, has not served a proposed response that is in substantial compliance with C.C.P. section 2033.220, and did not file an opposition to the motion. (C.C.P. section 2033.280.) Defendant demonstrated that Ms. Vlasyuk received proper and timely notice of both its requests for admission and its motion, which were served on plaintiff at the address identified by her former counsel as plaintiff's current address in the December 18, 2014 Order Granting Attorney's Motion To Be Relieved as Counsel, which order was served on Ms. Vlasyuk and made clear that she needed to keep the Court and the parties informed of her current address and phone number. No monetary sanctions are imposed against Ms. Vlasyuk. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Claudia Knap CSR #6099 of AikenWelch. (503/TLJ)
